raw_response:
<candidates>
<candidate_1>
<action>click('a80')</action>
<action_text>Click the funnel/filter icon to open the list filter builder</action_text>
<rationale>We need to create a filter where “Assigned to” is a specific user; opening the filter builder is the first step.</rationale>
</candidate_1>
<candidate_2>
<action>click('a78')</action>
<action_text>Click the adjacent list control icon to toggle/show the filter bar</action_text>
<rationale>If the primary funnel icon isn’t correct, this neighboring control often also reveals the filter panel in ServiceNow lists.</rationale>
</candidate_2>
<candidate_3>
<action>click('a119')</action>
<action_text>Click the list’s Search control</action_text>
<rationale>As a fallback, using the list search to enter the assignee’s name can quickly narrow results, helping us identify the correct record to extract the Warranty expiration.</rationale>
</candidate_3>
<candidate_4>
<action>click('a227', button='right')</action>
<action_text>Right-click the “Assigned to” column header to open its context menu</action_text>
<rationale>The column header context menu typically includes filtering options (e.g., Show Matching/Filter) that let us set “Assigned to” to a specific user.</rationale>
</candidate_4>
<candidate_5>
<action>click('a107')</action>
<action_text>Open the “Actions on selected rows…” list menu</action_text>
<rationale>This menu sometimes provides access to list controls, including showing the filter bar, if the filter icon is not functioning as expected.</rationale>
</candidate_5>
</candidates>

As a fallback, using the list search to enter the assignee’s name can quickly narrow results, helping us identify the correct record to extract the Warranty expiration.
